analysis.consistency: The spell's mechanics (neutralizing existing poison, granting advantage on saves, and granting resistance to poison damage) are consistent with its name and school of magic (Abjuration). The listed classes are appropriate for this level of protective magic.
analysis.quality: The description is clear and functionally complete. It precisely defines the immediate effect (neutralizing one poison) and the ongoing buff (advantage on saves, resistance to damage). The clarification on neutralizing one poison when multiple afflict the target is good quality.

mechanics.save_effect: Advantage on saving throws against being poisoned (for the duration)
mechanics.damage_effect: Resistance to poison damage (for the duration)
mechanics.target_effect: Neutralizes one poison afflicting the target upon casting.
mechanics.class_compatibility: Good fit for Cleric, Druid, Paladin, and Ranger.

analysis.notes: A solid, well-defined 2nd-level defensive spell. The components (V, S) align with standard protective magic.
